title: Megalithic Construction as QA Discrete Integer Arithmetic
_fixture_purpose: Certifies three lines of evidence that megalithic builders used discrete integer arithmetic consistent with QA: (1) Megalithic Yard as quantum of length, (2) Megalithic Fathom as operational unit, (3) Fibonacci QN construction triangles. Also documents three honest negative results.

megalithic_yard.source: Thom 1962 JRSS Tables 1&2 (84 circles) + Thom 1967 Table 5.1 (122 circles, 61 new) = 202 combined
megalithic_yard.method: Permutation null (100000 shuffles): mean fractional error of real diameters to nearest integer MY vs random diameters. Real=0.213, null=0.249±0.010, z=-3.54.
megalithic_yard.optimal_quantum: Tested 8 values [2.60, 3.00] ft. MY=2.72 gives minimum variance (0.0497). Sharp minimum — 2.70 close second (0.0554), 2.75 already worse (0.0596).
megalithic_yard.prior_result_84: At n=84 (1962 only): p=0.0096, z=-2.34. The 202-circle result is 10x more significant.
megalithic_yard._note: Independently confirms Thom's 1955/1962 claim using a different statistical method than his original Broadbent quantum detection. z=-3.54 corresponds to 99.98% confidence.

megalithic_fathom.method: Binomial test: 150/202 (74.3%) circles have even nearest-MY values. Under uniform: expected 50%. p < 10^-8.
megalithic_fathom._note: The Megalithic Fathom (2 MY) was the PRIMARY construction unit. MY is the half-unit. The 74.3% rate (vs 83.3% at n=84) reflects the 1967 book including more small circles that use half-fathom radii, which Thom himself noted.

construction_triangles.label: 3:4:5 triangle
construction_triangles._note: QN = first four Fibonacci numbers (1,1,2,3). The most common megalithic construction triangle. Used at Carnac, Stonehenge, Brodgar.

construction_triangles.label: 5:12:13 triangle
construction_triangles._note: QN = fundamental QA number (1,2,3,5) — Fibonacci. Second most common megalithic triangle. Direction (d,e)=(3,2): C=12, F=5, G=13.

construction_triangles.label: 8:15:17 triangle
construction_triangles._note: QN (3,1,4,5): NOT all-Fibonacci (3 is Fibonacci, 4 is not). Less commonly used in megalithic construction. Berggren tree M_C child of (2,1).

honest_negatives._note: 17 same-site pairs tested. Diameter ratios don't preferentially cluster near Fibonacci fractions.
MY values are uniformly distributed mod 9. No QA orbit structure in the diameters themselves.

Mod-24 distribution is non-uniform — but this is entirely explained by the fathom preference (83% even). Within even residues, the distribution reflects preferred fathom multiples, not independent mod-24 structure.

qa_connection.discrete_arithmetic: Megalithic builders used discrete integer arithmetic: fathom-based measurement → integer multiples → exact Pythagorean triangles. This IS the QA framework: discrete states, integer computation, no continuous approximation.
qa_connection.fibonacci_preference: The two most common construction triangles have all-Fibonacci QN tuples. Only 19% of primitive triples (hyp≤100) qualify. Same Fibonacci preference as orbital mean-motion resonances (cert [163]).
qa_connection.thom_crowhurst_pipeline: Thom (measurement/MY) → Crowhurst (Carnac harmonics/mod-12) → Wildberger (rational geometry) → QA (discrete dynamics). Four independent discoveries of the same discrete-arithmetic framework.
qa_connection.proto_navigation: Megalithic sites are proto-gnomonic stations. The 3:4:5 triangle enables right-angle surveying (the gnomonic tangent plane). The MY is the base scale. The fathom is the operational unit. Combined: a discrete navigation system predating all written records.
qa_connection.theorem_nt: The MY is the observer projection: continuous ground distance → discrete integer MY. Between measurements: exact integer arithmetic. Boundary crossed exactly twice per measurement.

sources: Thom, 'The Megalithic Unit of Length', JRSS Series A 125(2), 1962
Barnatt & Moir, 'Stone Circles and Megalithic Mathematics', Proc Prehist Soc (1984)
